Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Find Marcus's email to establish the correct AWS figures
Use search_emails (query: "AWS increase" or "$42,870" or "three times monthly expenditure"). Find Marcus's email (from: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, to: samira.tariq@moveops.com, CC: elena.rostova@moveops.com, dated March 30) where Marcus flagged the AWS invoice of $42,870 as nearly three times the usual monthly expenditure of approximately $14,300. This is the authoritative source establishing the correct 3x increase and baseline figures for cross-referencing all of Samira's subsequent replies.
OE 2: Find Samira's first error: email to Elena stating 2x increase instead of 3x
Use search_emails (query: "AWS cost increased two times" or "roughly two times Elena" or "Samira AWS reply"). Find email ID 0f9e2340c936484498f2d52425d3968a where Samira told Elena the AWS cost increased roughly two times. This is the first error; the correct figure is three times.
OE 3: Find Samira's second error: email to Elena with wrong RDS cost
Use search_emails (query: "EC2 S3 RDS breakdown Elena" or "final cost breakdown" or "RDS $892"). Find email ID 5677b0b27a044f3e8662b55d0670cda1 where Samira stated RDS Database cost as $892. This is the second error; the correct figure is $8,920.
OE 4: Find Samira's third error: recommended actions email missing alert thresholds
Use search_emails (query: "recommended actions engineering" or "infrastructure cost estimator" or "enterprise pricing model"). Find email ID c89017fd03f042cb9d439b2002a398dc where Samira listed only three recommended actions and omitted the immediate action of setting up AWS cost alert thresholds at 50%, 100%, and 200% of the baseline. This is the third error.
OE 5: Find Samira's fourth error: email to Marcus with wrong figures
Use search_emails (query: "Marcus AWS cost breakdown Vantage" or "AWS Cost Breakdown Marcus" or "RDS $892 Marcus"). Find email ID bd548d6bdbcf4fba95118fe08806a44f, where Samira told Marcus the cost increased roughly two times, and stated RDS as $892, repeating both mistakes as a fourth error.
OE 6: Find Marcus's meeting request and verify calendar event was never created
Use search_emails (query: "Marcus meeting May 1" or "calendar invite AWS" or "review numbers Marcus"). Find email ID 2af079c5d2224e28ad65674f7804f720 where Marcus asked Samira to create a meeting on May 1, 2026, 10:00-11:00 AM. Then use calendar_search_events (query: "Marcus AWS meeting" or "May 1 Samira Marcus" or "cost breakdown review") to confirm no calendar event was created, establishing the fifth mistake.
OE 7: Find Samira's sixth error: audit results with wrong Vectral false approvals
Use search_emails (query: "ExpenseBot audit results Elena" or "47 expenses" or "false approvals Vectral"). Find email ID 5bf2137624f24cecae3170f39fe61854, where Samira told Elena Vectral had 3 false approvals. This is the sixth error; Vectral had 0 false approvals, and all 3 were from Mosaic.
OE 8: Find Elena's unanswered technical question about Category Matcher
Use search_emails (query: "Category Matcher weighted scoring" or "Elena technical question" or "scoring mechanism keywords"). Find email ID 96076986a5e1403f94c0fa3021f9df2d where Elena asked whether the Category Matcher uses weighted scoring. Then, verify no reply exists from Samira, establishing the seventh mistake.
OE 9: Find Elena's pushback email on AWS cost alert threshold specification
Use search_emails (query: "Elena AWS alert threshold" or "5% increase threshold" or "early warning AWS" or "baseline alert Elena"). Find Elena's email to Samira where she specifies a 5% increase above baseline as the threshold for AWS cost alerts, explains the rationale as early warning to enable quick action before costs spiral, and requests a timeline and resource needs from Samira. This is the pushback on the recommendations that the prompt asks about.
OE 10: Check Slack for new auto-categorizer flags since the last audit
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "auto-categorizer flag" or "expense tool issue" or "new rejection" or "new approval" or "categorizer new"). Find whether any team member has posted about new issues with the expense tool since the audit was completed. Expected result: no new flags found.
OE 11: Check Slack for discussion about new pilot clients
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "pilot client" or "auto-categorizer new client" or "expense tool expansion" or "new pilot"). Find the recent mention of adding new pilot clients. Expected result: no new clients added; the original two remain Vectral Systems (AM: Mina Hashimoto) and Mosaic Robotics (AM: Emeka Diallo).
OE 12: Check Slack #root-cause-aws-spike for AWS recommendation statuses
Use conversations_history (channel: C009) or conversations_search_messages (query: "cost alert threshold" or "CloudWatch alert" or "cost tagging" or "per customer tag" or "AWS recommendations update"). Find Samira's summary message in channel C009 (#root-cause-aws-spike) about implementation statuses. Expected result: AWS cost alert thresholds currently in progress (ETA: Wednesday), cost tagging per customer currently in progress (ETA: Wednesday), pricing model review meeting scheduled for next week, and infrastructure cost estimation tool at P1 priority with a 2-week timeline.
OE 13: Check Linear for Receipt Detection ENG-220 status and first layer update
Use linear_list_issues (query: "ENG-220" or "receipt detection" or "hash fingerprint" or "duplicate detection" or "first layer"). Find the ENG-220 ticket (assignee: Dmitri Volkov, state: To Do) tracking the receipt hash-check system and check whether the assignee has posted any new updates on the implementation of the first layer. The only comment on ENG-220 is Dmitri's initial filing context from the ticket creation date, which states his intent to begin Layer 1 but is not a progress update on actual implementation. The ticket remains in To Do state. Expected result: no new implementation progress update from the assignee on the first layer.
OE 14: Search emails and Slack for Elena and Marcus comments on the first layer implementation
Use search_emails (query: "receipt detection first layer" or "ENG-220 Elena" or "hash fingerprint Marcus" or "duplicate detection leadership"). Also use conversations_search_messages (query: "receipt detection" or "first layer" or "ENG-220 leadership"). Find whether Elena or Marcus have commented on or responded to the first layer implementation. Expected result: neither Elena nor Marcus have commented.
OE 15: Look up Marcus Thorne and Elena Rostova contacts
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"), (query: "Elena Rostova"). Get marcus.thorne@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 16: Send corrected email to Elena fixing the 2x→3x error and RDS cost
Use send_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Body must: correct the AWS cost increase from roughly two times to three times, and correct the RDS Database cost from $892 to $8,920.
OE 17: Send a corrected email to Elena fixing the missing recommended action
Use send_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: elena.rostova@moveops.com). The body must include the omitted immediate recommended action, which is to set up AWS cost alert thresholds at 50%, 100%, and 200% of the baseline.
OE 18: Send corrected email to Elena fixing the Vectral false approvals audit error
Use send_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Body must: correct the audit table by stating Vectral Systems had 0 false approvals (not 3), and that all 3 false approvals were from Mosaic Robotics only.
OE 19: Reply to Elena's unanswered technical question about Category Matcher
Use send_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Body must answer Elena's question about whether the Category Matcher uses weighted scoring, explaining the scoring mechanism and how weights are applied to keywords or factors in edge cases.
OE 20: Send corrected email to Marcus fixing the 2x→3x error and RDS cost
Use send_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Body must: correct the AWS cost increase from roughly two times to three times, and correct the RDS Database cost from $892 to $8,920.
OE 21: Create a missing calendar event for Marcus meeting
Use calendar_add_calendar_event. The event must be on May 1, 2026, 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM, with marcus.thorne@moveops.com and samira.tariq@moveops.com as attendees, and the title referencing AWS cost breakdown review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via `send_email`) from `samira.tariq@moveops.com` to `elena.rostova@moveops.com` correcting the AWS cost increase multiple and the RDS Database cost.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✓✗ 17%
✗Run #1No email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com correcting AWS cost figures. The agent only sent two emails: one to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com and one to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com. The agent concluded 'No factual inaccuracies found' and did not identify the errors in Samira's prior communications.
✗Run #2No email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com specifically correcting the AWS cost increase multiple and RDS Database cost. The agent sent the RDS/$8,920 and 3x correction only to marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The three emails sent to Elena covered: (1) audit table correction, (2) 5% threshold response, (3) Category Matcher reply — none of which explicitly corrected the $892→$8,920 RDS figure or the 2x→3x multiplier error.
✗Run #3No send_email or correction email to elena.rostova@moveops.com correcting AWS cost multiple and RDS figure was found in the trajectory. The agent identified the SENT folder but failed to act on the errors.
✗Run #4No email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com correcting the AWS cost multiple or RDS figure. The agent sent emails to mina.hashimoto, dmitri.volkov, lena.bjorkstrom, and marcus.thorne (for cost-absorption policy), but never to Elena correcting these figures.
✓Run #5A `send_email` was called with sender samira.tariq@moveops.com, recipients [elena.rostova@moveops.com], and CC [marcus.thorne@moveops.com]. The body explicitly corrects both the AWS cost multiplier and the RDS Database cost.
✗Run #6No email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com correcting AWS cost multiple or RDS figures. The only email to Elena in the trajectory was about the overdue Launch Readiness Checklist, which mentioned the false rejection count correction but nothing about AWS cost figures.
2 The email body (visible in `send_email` parameters) to `elena.rostova@moveops.com` correcting AWS figures states the cost increase was approximately three times the baseline (not two times).
outcome
 ✗✗✗—✓✗ 20%
✗Run #1No correction email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com at all. The agent failed to identify the 2x vs 3x error in Samira's communications to Elena, so no correction email with the correct 3x figure was produced.
✗Run #2No correction email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com stating the cost increase was three times (not two times). The 3x correction was only communicated to Marcus. The emails sent to Elena did not address the 2x vs 3x discrepancy.
✗Run #3No email was sent to Elena. The criterion cannot be satisfied since no correction email to Elena was created.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The send_email body to Elena states 'costs increased approximately threefold (~3x): $42,870 vs. a baseline of ~$14,300', explicitly correcting the prior 2x claim.
✗Run #6No correction email to elena.rostova@moveops.com mentioning a 3x (or three times) AWS cost increase was sent. The agent never identified this as an error Samira made.
3 The email body (visible in `send_email` parameters) to `elena.rostova@moveops.com` correcting AWS figures states the RDS Database cost for March was $8,920 (not $892).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✓✗ 17%
✗Run #1No correction email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com. The agent did not identify the $892 vs $8,920 RDS error in prior emails and made no correction.
✗Run #2No email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com stating the RDS Database cost was $8,920 (not $892). The RDS cost correction appeared only in the email to Marcus. Samira had sent Elena the $892 figure in email 5677b0b27a044f3e8662b55d0670cda1 but no correction email to Elena references $8,920.
✗Run #3No email was sent to Elena correcting the RDS figure to $8,920. The agent never sent any correction emails.
✗Run #4No email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com correcting the RDS figure. The agent never identified the $892 vs $8,920 error.
✓Run #5The send_email body to Elena states 'The correct figure is: RDS Database: $8,920', explicitly correcting the prior $892 figure.

✓Run #3The final response's AWS recommendations table correctly shows both P0 items (AWS cost alert thresholds and budget tags per customer) with status 'Lena acknowledged, said by Wednesday' — matching the Slack channel data retrieved by the agent.
✗Run #4The final response says '⚠️ No confirmation of completion — was ETA Wednesday in late March' rather than reporting both items are 'currently in progress' with Wednesday ETA per Samira's Slack update. The agent found the Slack data (tool_call_result_6.json shows the in-progress status) but mischaracterized it as unconfirmed rather than in-progress.
✗Run #5The final response does not report the AWS cost alert thresholds work and cost tagging per customer work as in progress with Wednesday ETA.
✓Run #6The final response's AWS table states both P0 items (AWS cost alert thresholds and budget tags per customer) are 'in progress' with 'ETA: Wednesday ~3/31.' The Slack data in tool_call_result_6 confirms these statuses with Wednesday ETA, and the agent accurately reported them.
